text.source_text: व्यपदेशादित्यनुवर्तते । बृहदारण्यके षष्ठे प्रपाठके ‘ कतम आत्मेति योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु हृ द्यन्तर्ज्योतिः पुरुषः ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ३ । ७) इत्युपक्रम्य भूयानात्मविषयः प्रपञ्चः कृतः । तत्किं संसारिस्वरूपमात्रान्वाख्यानपरं वाक्यम् , उतासंसारिस्वरूपप्रतिपादनपरमिति संशयः । किं तावत्प्राप्तम् ? संसारिस्वरूपमात्रविषयमेवेति । कुतः ? उपक्रमोपसंहाराभ्याम् । उपक्रमे ‘ योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु ’ इति शारीरलिङ्गात् । उपसंहारे च ‘ स वा एष महानज आत्मा योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ४ । २२) इति तदपरित्यागात् । मध्येऽपि बुद्धान्ताद्यवस्थोपन्यासेन तस्यैव प्रपञ्चनादित्येवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
text.transliteration_iast: vyapadeśādityanuvartate | bṛhadāraṇyake ṣaṣṭhe prapāṭhake ‘ katama ātmeti yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu hṛ dyantarjyotiḥ puruṣaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 7) ityupakramya bhūyānātmaviṣayaḥ prapañcaḥ kṛtaḥ | tatkiṃ saṃsārisvarūpamātrānvākhyānaparaṃ vākyam , utāsaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparamiti saṃśayaḥ | kiṃ tāvatprāptam ? saṃsārisvarūpamātraviṣayameveti | kutaḥ ? upakramopasaṃhārābhyām | upakrame ‘ yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu ’ iti śārīraliṅgāt | upasaṃhāre ca ‘ sa vā eṣa mahānaja ātmā yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 4 | 22) iti tadaparityāgāt | madhye'pi buddhāntādyavasthopanyāsena tasyaiva prapañcanādityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —

translation.literal_translation: The phrase 'because of indication' continues. In the sixth chapter of the Brhadaranyaka, beginning with 'which is the Self? - this consciousness-formed person among the pranas, the inner light in the heart,' an extended Self-discourse is given. Doubt arises: is the passage merely describing the transmigrating individual, or teaching the non-transmigrating reality? Prima facie it seems to describe only the transmigrating self, from beginning and ending markers: at the beginning, the bodily-sign expression 'this consciousness-formed one among the pranas'; at the end, retention of that same expression, 'this great unborn Self, this consciousness-formed one among the pranas.' Even in the middle, by discussing waking and related states, it appears to elaborate that same individual. In response we say -
translation.technical_translation: Opening purvapaksha for 1.3.42 argues sharīra/jiva reading by upakrama-upasamhara continuity and state-analysis references.
translation.interpretive_note: Sets up a jiva-vs-Ishvara hermeneutic split inside the Brhadaranyaka self discourse.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit suṣupti/utkranti and patyadi technical term choices in records 1.3.106-1.3.110.
